description: Production workflow for generating a modular Pet Studio kit without mixing asset styles.

order.notes: Ask which pet should define the room/prop/helper style before generating room or prop assets. Accept an existing hatch-pet package, create a new hatch-pet first, or use an explicit style-lock file.
Freeze perspective, palette, outline, room size, door positions, and rendering language from the chosen style source before generating assets.

order.output: hatch-pet package containing pet.json and spritesheet.webp
order.notes: Create the main project-owner pet as a normal hatch-pet atlas.
Copy the hatch-pet package into the kit and attach style-lock metadata.

order.output: one hatch-pet package per animated helper role
order.notes: Sub-agent pets that need animation should also be generated by hatch-pet, then registered as helper layers.
Generate or author modular 384x240 source room backgrounds and small props using the same style-lock. The 192x208 hatch-pet cell is only for fallback baking.
Block mismatched styleId, perspective, room module size, missing door features, wrong atlas size, and oversized props.
Composite room, props, main pet, and helper pet layers into a hatch-pet-compatible package.
Use hatch-pet validation and contact sheet QA before installing.
